reason: Marty publishes unsigned Credential Issuer Metadata. OID4VCI permits unsigned metadata; this optional signed-metadata module must remain skipped until a separately approved signed-metadata feature is introduced.
Marty does not advertise batch_credential_issuance. OID4VCI permits issuers to support only single-credential issuance; enable this module only with an explicitly reviewed batch issuance capability.

OID4VCI 1.0 §4.1.1 requires a pre-authorized code to be short lived and single-use. This runner module redeems one offer with two wallets, so it is only applicable to Marty’s authorization-code profile; multi-wallet issuance must not weaken replay protection in the pre-authorized-code profile.

Marty's active SD-JWT VC configuration requires a JWT proof but does not advertise key_attestations_required. Key attestation is an optional OID4VCI Appendix D.1 capability, so this negative test is inapplicable until that capability is explicitly introduced and tested.

Marty's active issuer profile does not advertise credential encryption. OID4VCI makes encrypted credential responses optional; enable this negative module only with an explicitly reviewed encryption capability and interoperability coverage.
